experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active J near the top in its spawn orientation. With an empty field, the main opportunity is to place pieces efficiently to build a flat stack and preserve flexibility for upcoming Z, T, L, and I pieces. Candidate moves: moving left or right starts positioning toward a side; rotating is unnecessary on an empty board because the current horizontal J is already easy to place; soft_drop or hard_drop would commit too early before lateral positioning. A good sub-goal is to move the J toward a wall for clean early stacking, so shifting left begins that plan while keeping future options open.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is empty aside from the active J near the top in its spawn orientation. With an empty field, the priority is efficient placement and keeping the stack flat while planning for upcoming Z,T,L,I pieces. A J piece fits well on a side, and since only one action is allowed now, the strongest progress toward placement is to move it horizontally toward an edge. Rotations are not yet necessary on an empty board, and dropping immediately would commit it near the center, which is less flexible. Moving left starts guiding the J toward the wall for a clean side placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active J near the top in its spawn orientation. With no existing stack or immediate threats, the goal is to place pieces efficiently while preserving flexibility for upcoming Z, T, L, I. A strong default is to move the J toward a side before locking it, since dropping immediately in the center creates awkward early structure. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit too early in a poor central spot; soft_drop also advances downward before positioning; rotations are unnecessary on an empty board right now; left or right both improve edge placement. I choose left to start shifting the J toward the wall for a cleaner opening.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J piece is high on an empty board near the left side. With an empty field, the main goal is to place it cleanly to preserve a flat stack and keep flexibility for upcoming Z, T, L, I pieces. Immediate horizontal moves or rotations are possible, but the most efficient action now is to commit the piece downward since there are no obstacles or threats; dropping it quickly avoids wasting turns and any orientation can still be handled on future pieces. Hard drop places the current J safely on the bottom, creating a simple low stack.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

There is no immediate threat because the board is mostly empty. The goal now is efficient early-game placement to keep the surface clean and set up future T, L, I, S pieces. Moving the Z piece left lets it be dropped onto the left-side J stack to interlock more usefully than leaving it near the center/right or rotating immediately. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it awkwardly on the empty floor; rotation is not necessary yet; right moves away from the only existing structure; soft_drop commits vertically too early. Best immediate step is to shift left toward the stack.

Goal is to place the L efficiently without creating overhangs, and moving right starts aligning it toward the central/right area where it can later drop into a cleaner fit, while there is ample time before locking. Rotating now is unnecessary, hard drop would be premature, and shifting further left is constrained and less useful.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high and centered-left with a very low current stack. Surface has a small left-side structure around rows 17-19: Z at far left, J at columns 1-3 bottom, and T around columns 3-5. No immediate threat from height, so priority is efficient placement to build a flat surface and avoid sealing holes. The current spawn orientation can likely be moved and dropped later, but the best immediate sub-goal is to start shifting the L toward the left where it can eventually fit against the uneven left stack more cleanly than staying center. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would strand the L high over the center and create unnecessary bumps; soft_drop commits downward before lateral adjustment; rotations are not yet necessary because horizontal travel is still available and orientation can be chosen later; right moves away from the useful left structure. Therefore moving left is the best setup action.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high on the board with no immediate danger; the stack below is low and concentrated on the left/center with open space to the right. Goal is to place this L efficiently to build a flatter surface and avoid creating inaccessible holes. Since the piece is still near the top, immediate horizontal positioning is more important than dropping now. Moving right shifts it toward the open side where future I/S/O pieces can fit more cleanly; rotating now is unnecessary because the current orientation is serviceable and there is ample time later.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high above a low, uneven stack concentrated on the left-center. Immediate threat is minimal since the board is very open. Goal now is to place the L efficiently to build a flatter foundation and avoid creating holes before the upcoming I,S,O pieces. From the current spawn, moving/rotating first is better than dropping immediately; shifting left can aim the L toward the left-side structure where it can later settle into the uneven area more cleanly. A hard/soft drop now would commit too early near center, and rotation is not yet necessary before horizontal alignment.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L near the upper left-middle with lots of empty space below; stack on the bottom has a T-centered bump and left-side Z/J structure, so there is no immediate danger. Goal is to place the L efficiently to keep the board flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming I,S,O,I pieces. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would lock a premature placement without horizontal adjustment; soft_drop also commits downward before aligning; rotations could prepare a vertical tuck, but first shifting right gives more access to the central area and keeps options open for later rotations/drops. Therefore moving right is the best setup action now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high above a low uneven stack concentrated on the left/center. Bottom surface has a notch around columns 2-4, and there is no immediate danger because stack height is low. Goal is to place the L efficiently to keep the board flat for upcoming I,S,O,I pieces. The current horizontal L can move toward the left notch; a single-step action should advance that setup. Among candidates, left moves the piece toward the useful landing zone, right moves away, rotations are premature without alignment, and dropping now would commit too early.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high on the left-center with a messy but low stack; bottom has a T and J structure plus Z on the left, and there is no immediate danger. Goal is to place the L to improve the left side and avoid creating holes while keeping flexibility for upcoming I,S,O,I. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would commit a poor high placement from current x; lateral movement is preferable. Rotations are not urgent because the current flat orientation can still be repositioned. Moving left starts aligning the L with the left-side terrain/gap and preserves future options before dropping.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high above the stack, currently in flat-up orientation over columns 0-2. The surface below has a low, uneven structure concentrated on the left, while the right side is mostly empty. My goal is to place the L efficiently without creating inaccessible holes and to set up the upcoming I/S/O pieces for clean stacking. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would lock the current horizontal L on the far left, which is acceptable but may be less flexible than shifting. Rotations are not urgent because there is ample space and no immediate collision threat. Moving right starts guiding the piece toward the open center/right where it can be placed more cleanly and preserves future options on subsequent turns. Therefore the best immediate action is to move right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L at upper-left of the stack area with lots of empty space. Surface has a T bump on the right and a J/Z structure on the left, leaving a shallow notch around columns 2-4. Goal is to place L to build a flatter foundation without creating overhangs, preserving options for upcoming I,S,O,I. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would place the current horizontal L high on the left-center and is acceptable; moving/rotating first could optimize, but with one action this turn the strongest progress is to advance placement downward. Soft_drop reduces uncertainty and keeps flexibility more than hard_drop if further adjustment may be needed, but hard_drop secures the piece efficiently into the open left-center well. Chosen action: hard_drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S at the top near center, with a relatively flat open field above a messy stack concentrated on the lower left/center and a horizontal I on row 16 creating a shelf. Immediate threat is low because the board is very open. Goal now is efficient placement to keep the surface clean and set up future O/I/T/L pieces. Candidate moves: hard_drop would commit the current horizontal S near the spawn columns, likely not ideal without alignment; soft_drop just advances without improving placement; rotate_right makes the S vertical, which is useful for tucking into the central-left notches around columns 1-3 above the Z/J area on later moves. Left/right could also start positioning, but rotating first preserves flexibility and matches the visible notch structure better. Best sub-goal is to orient the S vertically before shifting into a useful slot.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an S near the upper-left/center with plenty of vertical space before contacting the stack. The surface below has a right-side overhang around the T and I pieces, while the left side is uneven with Z/J/L. Immediate threats are low because the piece is high and the stack is only in the bottom rows. My goal now is to improve future placement flexibility and avoid committing too early with a hard drop. Comparing actions: left/right shift the S horizontally while it is still high, preserving options; rotation changes between vertical and horizontal S, but with no nearby collision yet there is no urgent need to rotate now. Dropping now would commit without enough alignment. Moving right steers the S toward the more open central-right lanes where it can later fit around the T/I structure or set up for the upcoming O/I/T pieces.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is O at the very top centered columns 4-5. Surface below is low and uneven, with a vertical L stem at column 3 around rows 14-15 and a long I on row 16 columns 4-7. Goal is to place the O to keep the stack flat and avoid creating holes while preserving flexibility for upcoming I,T,L,Z. Since O cannot rotate and is already well-positioned over the broad central area, the best immediate progress is to drop it quickly into a useful central gap. Horizontal moves are possible later, but from current spawn the center is safe and efficient.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I tetromino currently horizontal near the top at columns 3-6. The stack is concentrated low on the left/center, with a horizontal I already filling row 16 columns 4-7 and a mostly open board above. Immediate threat is low, so the goal is to place this I efficiently to set up future pieces and avoid wasting the long bar. A vertical I can fit the open 2-column well at x=2 (0-indexed) above the left stack, potentially smoothing the surface, but first we need to rotate. Candidate moves: left/right only shift the horizontal bar; soft/hard drop horizontal would likely create a flat but less flexible placement across open space. Rotating right now preserves high placement flexibility and is the best sub-goal.

Immediate goal is to place this I to improve future line clears and avoid creating overhangs. Rotating would make it horizontal high in the field, but first shifting is better; dropping now in column 5 would likely create an isolated tall pillar over existing gaps. Other actions are less useful now: no_op wastes time, right moves toward emptier but less needed area, and drop actions commit too early.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I horizontally at the top around columns 2-5, with option to rotate vertical at column 4. The stack is low and uneven near the bottom; there is a clear 4-wide horizontal gap on row 16 at columns 4-7 already filled by a settled I, so no immediate tetris well to exploit. Goal now is efficient placement without creating overhangs, ideally keeping the surface flat for upcoming T,L,Z,J. Since the piece is still high, committing with hard/soft drop in the current horizontal orientation would likely place it across the mostly empty field and can help maintain a flat top; rotating vertical this early is less useful and could create a spike. A small horizontal adjustment seems unnecessary because center placement is already flexible. Best immediate action is to hard drop the current horizontal I onto the empty upper field to lock it flat and preserve simplicity.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high near the center (around columns 4-6) with plenty of empty space below. The stack is uneven but the most notable feature is a T-shaped cavity in the lower-right center: row 17 has a single T at column 4, row 18 has TTT at columns 3-5, and row 19 is open above a J block at column 1-3, making columns 4-5 relatively accessible. My goal now is to steer the T toward a useful landing that fits the mid-lower structure rather than hard-dropping uselessly in the center. Candidate actions: hard_drop would waste the piece on top of the stack immediately; soft_drop just lowers without aligning; rotations don’t help yet because the spawn orientation is already flexible and there is no obstruction. Moving right starts positioning the T over the central-right cavity and keeps future options for rotation/open placement with next pieces L,Z,J,O. So the best immediate action is right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high and unobstructed, with the stack concentrated low on the left/center. The immediate goal is to position this T for a useful placement before it descends further, ideally toward the left/center where there are future fitting opportunities, while keeping flexibility for later L/Z/J/O pieces. A hard drop now would waste that flexibility by locking the T far above the stack. Rotating is not urgent because the current vertical orientation is already workable in open space. Moving right shifts away from the main stack and likely reduces future utility. Soft drop/no_op also give up a chance to improve horizontal alignment. Moving left starts bringing the T toward the central-left structure where it can later be rotated/dropped into a better slot.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T in rotation 1, currently high and slightly left of center. The stack is low overall with a noticeable left-side notch around rows 10-12 and a broad flat area over the middle from the existing I and O pieces. Immediate goal is to place the T efficiently without creating extra holes while setting up for upcoming L/Z/J. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would lock the current awkward orientation into the upper-left surface without adjustment; soft_drop also commits downward before improving alignment. Left moves toward the left notch but current column already aligns reasonably; right would move away from the cleaner fit. Rotating right to orientation 2 makes a flat T-top that can later sit well on uneven surfaces, but from the current position the better immediate setup is rotation left to orientation 0, which creates the classic T shape with center stem up and a 3-wide base, improving fit options over the left-middle contour and preserving flexibility before dropping. So the best single action now is to rotate left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high near the top with no immediate danger. The stack below has a broad flat area around rows 13–16 and some bumps/holes on the left-center; preserving flexibility is better than dropping the current horizontal L immediately. Rotating to vertical-first orientation can better target left-center notches on subsequent moves, while moving left/right now without rotation commits less usefully. Hard/soft drop now is premature from this height. So the best sub-goal is to prepare a more placeable orientation first.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L high above the stack with plenty of horizontal freedom. The surface below has a notable left-side notch/unevenness around rows 15-19, while the center-right is relatively flatter but not ready for this exact orientation yet. Goal is to improve future placement without committing too early. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would place immediately or advance downward before better horizontal alignment; rotations can wait since current vertical-L orientation is workable and there is space to adjust later. Moving left starts steering toward the more useful left-side terrain and preserves flexibility for later rotation/drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L in vertical orientation near the upper-left, with an entirely empty field beneath it until the existing stack starts much lower. There is no immediate threat, so the goal is to place this piece toward the left side to build cleanly without creating isolated holes. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately at the current column, which is premature; soft_drop just lowers without improving placement; rotations are not needed yet because the current vertical L can still be maneuvered during descent; right moves away from the useful left wall. Moving left now shifts the piece toward the left edge, preserving options for a wall placement on subsequent turns.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L in rotation 1, positioned high on the left with a completely open field beneath it in those columns. The stack below is low and uneven mainly in the center/right, so there is no immediate threat. Goal is to place the L efficiently on the left side without wasting time, likely to fill the left wall structure and keep the center accessible for upcoming Z,J,O,S. Since the current column under the piece is open all the way down and no lateral adjustment is urgently needed this turn, the strongest progress is to commit downward immediately. hard_drop places it at once, reducing risk and advancing the board state faster than soft_drop or movement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top. Current stack has a relatively flat open center-right upper field, while the left side has a tall L column. Immediate goal is to place the Z cleanly without creating holes and keep options open for upcoming J,O,S,S. At the current spawn, moving/rotating matters before committing downward. Rotation right changes the Z into vertical orientation around columns 5-6, which can be steered into future notches, but there is no immediate need to lock now. Shifting right from current horizontal spawn better aligns the Z over the open right-side surface and avoids building over the left column. Hard/soft drop now would likely place it high and less flexibly. So the best single action is to move right first.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the upper right, currently vertical-ish in rotation 1 occupying rows 6-8 and cols 8-9. The only valid shown rotations are current rotation 1 and rotation 0, so movement is constrained but there is open space below. Surface below the piece on the right side is very low compared to the left, so immediate goal is to place this Z efficiently without creating extra overhangs. Rotating right to rotation 0 would make the piece horizontal across the top and likely set up a cleaner descent into the right-side valley; moving sideways first is less urgent than locking downward while space is clear. soft_drop advances toward placement while keeping flexibility for later adjustments, whereas hard_drop may commit too early and horizontal moves can still be made after descending. So soft_drop is the best progress action now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a Z near the top-right, currently in vertical orientation occupying the right side above an uneven stack. The immediate goal is to place it safely while reducing holes and avoiding creating an overhang. Moving left would not meaningfully improve the surface right now, and rotating to horizontal would place it across row 8-9 over columns 7-9, which risks a worse fit above the existing right-side structure. Keeping the current vertical orientation and dropping into the existing notch on the far right is the cleanest short-term placement. A soft drop advances toward that fit while preserving adjustment options on the next turn; hard drop is riskier if the exact landing is suboptimal.

The stack is uneven but there is open space immediately to the left of the current Z position; rotating to horizontal would place blocks at row 10 cols 7-8 and row 11 cols 8-9, which creates a flatter placement option over the right side. Goal is to place this Z cleanly without creating a tall right-wall overhang and to set up upcoming J/O/S pieces. Among actions, rotate_right is best now because it changes the awkward vertical Z into the more useful horizontal orientation while still high enough to maneuver afterward. Left/right alone keep the less flexible shape; dropping now would likely commit to a worse cavity.

experiences.intentions: Active piece appears to be a T near the upper-middle/right, currently spanning row 12 with one block at row 11. The stack is uneven, with a useful notch on the right side around columns 7-9 and some holes lower left/mid that are not immediately fixable. Goal is to place the current T cleanly without creating overhangs and to set up the upcoming J/O/S/S pieces. Comparing actions: moving left heads into taller clutter; dropping immediately in current orientation likely leaves a less efficient surface. The shown clockwise rotation (Rotation 1) shifts the T into a vertical orientation that fits the right-side contour more naturally and can be followed by a drop on later turns. So rotate now is the best setup action.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a Z near the upper-right above a jagged stack. Current orientation appears vertical; rotating right would place it neatly into the right-side notch around rows 12-14, but rotation alone does not advance downward. Immediate goal is to preserve access to that fit and avoid drifting. Candidate actions: left/right would misalign from the right well; hard_drop now in current orientation would likely create a worse overhang; soft_drop advances toward the target landing while keeping the promising column and orientation options open. So the best single move now is to move the piece down one.

Goal is to place the J to improve future surface and avoid wasting the current safe turn. Immediate useful progress is horizontal movement toward a side rather than dropping blindly. Moving right shifts the J away from the left clutter and toward the open right side, preserving flexibility for later rotation/drop decisions.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near center-right with no immediate collision risk. Surface below has mixed stacks, including a left-side well and uneven center/right. Goal now is to place the J efficiently without committing to a bad early lock; with next pieces O,S,S,J, preserving flexibility is good. Candidate actions: hard_drop is too committal from spawn without aligning; soft_drop just lowers without improving fit; rotations don’t obviously target a strong slot yet. Shifting left starts moving the J toward the more open central-left area and keeps future rotation/drop options available.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high at the top with no immediate collision threat. The stack below is uneven, with a left-center cavity pattern and relatively open air above, so the main goal is to position the J for a useful placement before committing downward. From spawn, rotating right changes it into a vertical J that can more flexibly fit future wells; moving or dropping now would commit too early while still far from the stack. Among candidates, left/right only shifts laterally without improving fit yet, soft/hard drop are premature, and rotate_right gives a stronger orientation for navigation and placement.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near center-right in vertical orientation. Surface below is uneven but not threatening yet; best immediate objective is to move the piece toward a cleaner landing zone before committing. Shifting left improves access to the broad open center-left and keeps future rotations/placement options, while right moves toward the wall and hard/soft drop would commit too early. Rotation now does not clearly improve compared with first repositioning.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near center in vertical orientation. Surface below is uneven with a left well and cluttered right; immediate threat is low because stack is far from top, so goal is to improve placement rather than rush. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit current central column placement without lateral adjustment, likely creating awkward overhang. soft_drop only delays without improving alignment. Rotating could help later, but first shifting left moves the J toward the open left-center area where it can be placed more cleanly on upcoming turns. Right is less attractive because the right side is bumpier and constrained by Z blocks. Best sub-goal now is reposition before dropping.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is in rotation 1 at upper-left area with empty space below, so there is no immediate lock threat. Surface below has a left-side cavity and the current J could usefully tuck left to align with the existing left stack rather than dropping in place onto the flatter center-right. Goal is to improve future placement before committing, since next pieces O,S,S,J favor preserving central access. Candidate actions: hard/soft drop would commit prematurely in a suboptimal column; rotations don't create a clearly better immediate fit than the current shape; moving right heads toward rougher terrain. Moving left shifts toward the left well and keeps options open for a later drop/rotation tuck.

experiences.intentions: Active J is in rotation 1 at upper-left/mid-left, floating with room to maneuver. Surface below is jagged, with a relatively open left side and upcoming O,S,S,J suggesting we should avoid locking a bad high placement. Comparing moves: left shifts toward the wall where J can later tuck; right moves away from the available left channel; rotations don't yet create a clearly better fit than repositioning first. Best sub-goal is to move left to line up a future drop into the left-side cavity.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is O at the very top occupying columns 4-5, rows 0-1. O has no meaningful rotation, so rotate actions do nothing useful. Stack is uneven but there is a clean vertical lane under the O piece in those same columns for many rows; moving sideways would aim for distant surfaces but wastes time and may create awkward overhangs. Immediate goal is to place the O efficiently and safely to continue building without introducing holes. Hard dropping now will send the O straight down into the open central well, locking it in a reasonable low position and progressing fastest toward line-building.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top (rows 1-2), currently horizontal with a valid vertical rotation. The stack is relatively low and uneven, with no immediate danger at the spawn area. Goal now is to place the S efficiently while preserving flexibility; rotating now gives a vertical S that can more easily fit into future notches as we descend, and there is no downside since space is clear. experiences.intentions: The active piece is an S near the upper middle-right, currently in vertical orientation. The stack below is uneven with some holes, but there is no immediate top-out threat because the piece is still high and the board is relatively low in the center-right. My goal now is to place the S to improve surface smoothness and avoid creating extra overhangs. Rotating to horizontal would create a flatter placement option over the center-right contours, while moving immediately left/right without rotation is less committal and dropping now in vertical orientation is likely to create awkward bumps. So the best immediate setup action is to rotate the S clockwise into its horizontal orientation.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top with current horizontal placement around columns 5-7. The stack below is uneven but there is no immediate danger at the top, so the main goal is to place this S efficiently to avoid creating overhangs and prepare for upcoming J/O/I pieces. Direct hard drop from here would likely leave the S on the right side without aligning to a clear slot; rotating vertical could better fit into narrow spaces later, but from the current high position the most useful immediate setup is to shift toward a better landing area before dropping. Moving right pushes the S toward the relatively open right half, where its shape can stack more cleanly above the existing right-side blocks and preserve flatter surfaces than staying or rotating now.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an S near the upper-right, currently horizontal with two blocks on row 11 cols 8-9 and two on row 12 cols 7-8. The surface below/right is jagged, with an existing Z stack at cols 8-9 around rows 13-15 and open space to the far right. Immediate goal is to place the S to improve the right-side contour without creating overhangs. Rotating right would make the S vertical at cols 8-9, which better matches the notch above the Z stack and can descend cleanly on subsequent moves. Moving left/right first is less useful before aligning the shape; hard drop now would lock the current horizontal placement too high and awkwardly. So rotate now.

Immediate threats are low since the top is mostly empty. Left/right or rotation are premature without a clear target, and hard drop would commit too early. Soft dropping safely progresses toward placement.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the upper-right with ample vertical space below and some uneven terrain lower down. Immediate threats are low because the stack is only in the bottom half, so the priority is improving placement flexibility rather than locking now. Rotating right changes the S from horizontal to vertical, which can better fit into right-side notches and preserves options before committing with lateral movement or drop. Left/right without rotating is less informative, and dropping now risks a poor surface match.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the upper-right with its current vertical orientation occupying around columns 6-7. The surface below on the right has a useful notch near columns 7-9 above existing S/Z blocks, and immediate danger is low because the stack is only mid-height. Goal is to place this S efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally preparing for upcoming J/O/I/T. Rotating right would switch to horizontal and can set up a cleaner tuck onto the right-side contour; moving left/right first is less urgent than getting the favorable orientation, and dropping now in vertical form is less flexible. So the best immediate action is to rotate the S clockwise.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the upper-middle left, currently around columns 5-7. Surface below has a central notch and some unevenness, but no immediate top-out threat since stack height is moderate. Goal is to place the S efficiently to reduce gaps and preserve flexibility for upcoming J,O,I,T. Candidate actions: rotate_right would make the S vertical-ish and less useful here; left/right shift could aim for another slot but needs more setup; hard_drop now would lock the current horizontal S into a reasonable spot over the central area, covering row 6-7 cells and avoiding unnecessary delay. Best sub-goal is safe placement with minimal holes and keeping the board open.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the top with open air below; surface is uneven with a left-side column and center-right gaps. Immediate goal is to start moving/settling the J toward a useful placement rather than stalling at spawn. Hard drop now would likely place it high and awkwardly; rotation is not yet necessary because there is room and no urgent fit. Moving right shifts the J toward the more open center-right area, preserving future rotations and drops for cleaner placement.

experiences.intentions: The active J piece is high near the center-right with lots of vertical space below. The stack is uneven but not near top-out, and there is no immediate threat. My goal now is to move the J toward a useful landing zone instead of locking it high in the current column. Rotating now does not create an urgent benefit because there is ample time, and dropping now would commit to a mediocre placement near the center. Moving left starts shifting the piece toward the broader open area over the left-center where J can likely tuck against existing steps on later turns.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high and unobstructed. Surface below has a notable left-side cavity/column structure around rows 6-10, while center-right is relatively busy but not urgent. Goal is to place the J efficiently without creating overhangs, and since next pieces are O,I,T,Z, preserving flatter center access is useful. Immediate best sub-goal is to move the J left toward the open left-center so it can later drop into a cleaner slot; rotating now is premature and hard-dropping from spawn would waste flexibility.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the top at x≈3 in horizontal orientation. Surface below is uneven with open space under the current piece, so there is no immediate lock threat. Goal is to place J to improve the left/center skyline without creating overhangs; with next pieces O,I,T,Z, preserving flexibility is good. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately from a poor central spawn position; soft_drop lowers without horizontal setup; rotations here mostly make a vertical J but still need positioning. Moving right heads toward the broad central-right well and buys setup time, while avoiding the crowded left stack.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active J is high near the center-top in spawn orientation. The stack is very uneven with open space near the top, so there is no immediate lock danger. Goal is to place the J efficiently to avoid creating overhangs and set up future O/I/T/Z placements. From the shown rotations, rotating right to orientation 1 makes the J vertical with a foot to the left, which is generally more flexible for sliding into side wells; moving before dropping is likely better than committing now. Among single moves, rotate_right improves future placement options more than left/right/soft_drop, while hard_drop would commit too early.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the top with open space below-right and a somewhat uneven stack. Immediate threats are low since the stack is far from the top; the goal is to place the J cleanly without creating overhangs and set up for upcoming O/I/T. A hard drop now in the current orientation is safe and efficient, likely landing the J onto the upper surface around the center-left without wasting a move. L/R or rotation could refine placement, but with limited evidence of a clearly superior slot, securing the piece quickly is the best sub-goal.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is O at the very top centered columns 4-5. O has no meaningful rotation change, and the preview shows no other valid rotated shape. The board surface near the middle has a T around rows 11-12 creating a shallow notch; dropping the O now is the main opportunity. Goal is to place the current O efficiently and move to next I/T/Z/L sequence. Left/right could fine-tune, but with only one move now the strongest progress is to commit placement. Hard drop sends the O into the available cavity around rows 9-10 / 10-11 near columns 4-5, improving stack compactness faster than soft drop or no_op.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I horizontally near the top. The stack has a deep vertical well around column 3–4 in the lower half and some uneven surfaces; preserving flexibility and getting the I vertical is valuable because a vertical I can be dropped into narrow wells for a clean line setup. Immediate danger is low since the piece is at the top with plenty of space. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place the I horizontally on the upper surface and likely create more overhangs; left/right without rotating keeps it horizontal and less useful; soft_drop commits downward before orienting. Rotating right turns the I vertical immediately, best aligning with the useful wells and keeping future placement options open.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.
